$comment: Machine-readable spec of the loop-vibe-coding state-machine table. An AI agent (via any Feishu/Lark Bitable API or MCP) can consume this to create the table deterministically. Field 'name' values are the DEFAULT column names; they map to the logical keys in loop.config.yaml > base.fields. After creation, write the resulting app_token and table_id back into loop.config.yaml.

table.name: loop-vibe-coding
table.description: One row per change. The `owner` field is the relay baton between coder and reviewer; the `status` field is the lifecycle stage.

fields.name: change_id
fields.desc: OpenSpec change identifier, filled after the spec is written.

fields.name: status
fields.desc: Lifecycle stage. Agents may never set 'done'.

fields.name: spec_ref
fields.desc: Spec content or a link to it.

fields.name: review
fields.desc: Structured, severity-tagged feedback. Appended per round.

fields.name: resolution
fields.desc: Per-item judgement of feedback + what was changed + checks run.

fields.name: round
fields.desc: Review iteration counter. Drives the max_rounds circuit breaker.

fields.name: test_report
fields.desc: Machine test/lint output + the human's acceptance notes.

fields.name: bug
fields.desc: Repro steps logged when bouncing a change back from testing.

fields.name: updated_at
fields.desc: Auto timestamp; pollers pick the oldest pending record.

views.name: Needs me
views.desc: Your inbox: rows awaiting acceptance test or a tie-break.

post_create: Write the created base app_token into loop.config.yaml > base.app_token.
Write the created table_id into loop.config.yaml > base.table_id.
If any column name differs from the defaults above, update loop.config.yaml > base.fields accordingly.
Optional: in Feishu Bitable, add an automation that sends a message when `owner` becomes `human`.
